title: Coordinates, Scalars, Vector2, Angles, and Units · Behavior Case: Motion Units
summary: The ship stores integer x and y, mixes degrees with radians, normalizes a zero vector, treats screen y as mathematical y, and adds pixels per second directly to position. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the motion units increment in unit-labeled 2D geometry model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice…
objectives: Label position, velocity, acceleration, angle, angular velocity, seconds, and frame counts and reject unit-invalid formulas.
Guard normalization and scale-to-length operations when a vector has no direction.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-coordinate-vector-units-5
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-coordinate-vector-units-5.
# Case 9036: a controller-remapping accessibility review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def worked_asteroids_coordinate_vector_units_5(x: float = 3.0, y: float = 4.0, pixels_per_second: float = 120.0, seconds: float = 0.25, zero_normalized: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "playerworked5",
        "seed9036",
        "input5dekrow",
        "frameworked59036",
        "state9036worked5",
        "playtest5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    magnitude = (x * x + y * y) ** 0.5
    if magnitude <= 0.0:
        return False, magnitude, "zero-vector"
    if pixels_per_second < 0.0 or seconds < 0.0:
        return False, seconds, "unit-domain"
    if zero_normalized:
        return False, magnitude, "zero-normalization"
    displacement = pixels_per_second * seconds
    assert abs(magnitude - 5.0) < 1e-9
    return True, displacement, "explicit-units"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python Asteroids decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected frame, input, seam, collision, cue, save, package, or release case, then state which native pygame-ce, SDL, display, controller, audio, filesystem, GPU, accessibility playtest, installer, performance, recovery, or publication claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-motion-units-2vskb
# Competency: asteroids-motion-units.
# Case 3463: a headless continuous-integration replay.
# Operating constraint: test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases.
# Changed case: run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save.
def evidence_asteroids_motion_units_2vskb(x: float = 3.0, y: float = 4.0, pixels_per_second: float = 120.0, seconds: float = 0.25, zero_normalized: bool = False) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_2vskb = "2vskb-bksv2-3463"
    assert evidence_variant_2vskb
    evidence_axes_2vskb = (
        "player2vskb",
        "seed3463",
        "inputbksv2",
        "frame2vskb3463",
        "state34632vskb",
        "playtestbksv23463",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_2vskb)) == 6
    magnitude = (x * x + y * y) ** 0.5
    if magnitude <= 0.0:
        return False, magnitude, "zero-vector"
    if pixels_per_second < 0.0 or seconds < 0.0:
        return False, seconds, "unit-domain"
    if zero_normalized:
        return False, magnitude, "zero-normalization"
    displacement = pixels_per_second * seconds
    assert abs(magnitude - 5.0) < 1e-9
    return True, displacement, "explicit-units"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Motion Units model · Theory Motion Units
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Motion Units: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 9562 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile screen coordinates, Vector2 magnitude and direction, angle convention, and explicit motion units; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Label position, velocity, acceleration, angle, angular velocity, seconds, and frame counts and reject unit-invalid formulas.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Motion Units model · Theory Motion Units.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Motion Units model · Theory Motion Units, keep Motion Units connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Label position, velocity, acceleration, angle, angular velocity, seconds, and frame counts and reject unit-invalid formulas.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Motion Units model · Theory Motion Units, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Motion Units precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Motion Units model · Theory Motion Units, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 9562 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile screen coordinates, Vector2 magnitude and direction, angle convention, and explicit motion units; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Label position, velocity, acceleration, angle, angular velocity, seconds, and frame counts and reject unit-invalid formulas.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Motion Units model · Theory Motion Units, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Multiplying or dividing by delta time is optional style.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: asteroids-motion-units-2vskb.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: asteroids-motion-units-2vskb(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_asteroids_motion_units_2vskb\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?magnitude <= 0.0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?pixels_per_second < 0.0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?zero_normalized)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?pixels_per_second \* seconds)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
